title: Graph (Node-Link) Accessibility Model
summary: Tier-2 grounding for the webgraph standard (epic #1351, shape #1352 fork 3): how to make a node-link diagram accessible beyond the ratified tier-1 floor (an adjacency description-table, mirroring webcharts' a11y-description-table case). Graph a11y has no settled platform pattern — a graph is neither a list nor a tree, so the ARIA treegrid/treeview pattern fits only partially and the WAI-ARIA Graphics Module (graphics-document / graphics-object / graphics-symbol) names roles without prescribing traversal. The strongest prior art is structure-based keyboard navigation generated from the spec: Olli (UW IDL) renders a keyboard-navigable ARIA tree/treegrid alongside a viz, Data Navigator (Elavsky et al.) generalizes structure-driven node/edge traversal, Chartability is the rubric to score against, and Highcharts' a11y module is the mature commercial reference (keyboard graph navigation + screen-reader regions + sonification). Recommendation to spec later: tier-2 = WAI-ARIA Graphics roles on the SVG render + a structure-derived keyboard traversal model (focus a node → arrow to neighbours) generated from GraphSpec adjacency, with sonification a tier-3 additive — NOT decided here; this topic grounds the eventual webgraph conformance a11y axis.
